title: astragalus cracca
intro: Astragalus cracca, also known as milk vetch or white milk vetch, is a perennial legume native to the mountainous regions of North America and Europe. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, containing saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its health benefits. Astragalus cracca is traditionally used to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, or taken as capsules or powders, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
